name: Multi-tier reference architecture
existence_evidence: Each tier has independent products (Redis, Qdrant, GPTCache, LMCache, provider APIs). Introl and ZeroEntropy document multi-tier prefix+semantic patterns in 2025. No single vendor bundles all five tiers with unified invalidation.
gap_vs_vision: Unified write-back to all tiers async, single doc_corpus_version invalidating T1-T3 together, and cross-tool gateway enforcement.
summary: Composable pipeline: Tier 0 normalize → T1 exact → T2 semantic answer → T3 retrieval → T4 prefix KV → LLM; async populate allowed tiers on miss.
how_it_works: Request flows cheapest-first. Misses fall through. Response worker writes to tiers permitted by classification. Invalidation: model change busts all; corpus_version busts T1-T3; prefix break busts T4 only.
integration_points: Ingress gateway, RAG service, vector DB, Redis, inference engine, message queue for write-back and audit.
correctness_risks: Tier ordering wrong (semantic before retrieval) wastes money. Writing personal answers to org lane.
cost_latency_impact: Stacking tiers compounds hit probability; T3+T4 often dominate RAG savings with lower risk than T2 alone.
environmental_impact: Multi-tier maximizes compute avoidance per request.
pii_and_compliance_controls: Per-tier write matrix: T3 org-wide ok with version keys; T2 requires scrub+lane; T4 only if prefix has no user-specific bytes.
org_scale_patterns: One architecture diagram, many deployables; shared observability dashboard for hit rate by tier and lane.
rollout_priority: Design all tiers; ship T3+T4 first, then T1, then T2
makes_sense_rationale: Matches industry direction and user clarification; integration is the product.
